description: Basic sentence structure and parts of speech

topics.description: Nouns, verbs, adjectives, adverbs, prepositions, conjunctions, interjections

rules.rule: Nouns name people, places, things, or ideas.
rules.ielts_usage: Use specific nouns in Academic Writing for clarity.

rules.rule: Verbs express actions or states of being.
rules.ielts_usage: Use precise verbs to strengthen Task 2 arguments.

medium: The diligent student consistently completes assignments.
Environmental pollution has become a global concern.
ielts: Government investment in public transportation reduces traffic congestion and lowers carbon emissions.
The rapid development of technology has transformed communication methods significantly.
academic: The implementation of evidence-based pedagogical approaches enhances student learning outcomes.
A comprehensive analysis of demographic trends reveals significant socio-economic implications.

common_mistakes.correct: He is good at playing guitar.

rules.rule: Every complete sentence must have a subject and a predicate.
rules.ielts_usage: Avoid sentence fragments in academic writing.

medium: The research team conducted an extensive literature review.
Urban planning requires careful consideration of multiple factors.
ielts: Effective time management enables students to balance academic and personal responsibilities successfully.
The proliferation of social media platforms has fundamentally altered interpersonal communication patterns.
academic: Methodological rigor in empirical research necessitates transparent documentation of data collection procedures.
Conceptual frameworks provide theoretical scaffolding for analyzing complex social phenomena.

common_mistakes.correct: The event was cancelled because of the bad weather.
